"Mrs. Santa Lu Hemer, MSNP candidates, is what we call them"; canonical MFDP (Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party) — recurring catalog pattern // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
"I sent it out on MFDP letterhead. And I hadn't thought about it"; later "It was sent out on the MFBP letterhead as a..."; canonical MFDP // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

pending_items.whisper_rendering: Joe Roe / Joe Ro / Joe Rao (recurring)
pending_items.correction: Joseph Rauh / Joe Rauh (Joseph L. Rauh Jr.)
pending_items.notes: "you better discuss it with Joe Roe. And Bob said, well, I just left Joe Roe's office. And Joe said we could be seated"; canonical Joseph L. Rauh Jr. (1911–92), UAW general counsel, ADA co-founder, key DC civil-rights lobbyist; argued the MFDP credentials case at the 1964 Atlantic City convention // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"And we recruited Rad Featherstone. Now Feather..."; canonical Ralph Featherstone (SNCC field secretary; assistant director of Freedom Schools 1964; killed in 1970 Bel Air MD car-bomb with Che Payne); deserves ground-truth corpus entry // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"we'd saw it at the clothes and we'd get boxes... So they used to come in"; "the Stiklpande safe way and stop and shop"; canonical SNCC's clothing-and-food drives — Whisper rendering "Snick offices" as "Stiklpande" // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending] // [LAYER-5: D3-catalog-contradiction (catalog 'B-ext': 'SNCC'), ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"Guy from Wisconsin, Castanmar, Senator Bob Castanmar from Wisconsin. The most interesting thing is that at Edita was standing up to this SNCC book, she married one of Castanmar's sons or something like that"; canonical Robert W. Kastenmeier (1924–2015), Democratic Congressman (not senator) from Wisconsin's 2nd district 1959–91; voting-rights ally // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"very dignified waspegai, who looked like central caste"; speaker's intersectional descriptor — Paul Moore was a white WASP and was bisexual (publicly disclosed posthumously by daughter Honor Moore) // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
Same as #34.P2T.41 // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"we got in the door because of Goodman, Shunian, Traynor, because we took all those things to the National State Conventions"; canonical Goodman/Schwerner/Chaney — recurring catalog pattern // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"Walter Ruther sent out a telegram"; "called this meeting and gets who he brings to speak for him. But of course, got bad always"; canonical Walter Reuther (1907–70), UAW president 1946–70, AFL-CIO vice president // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

Pass 3 confirms this is a new catalog-#B candidate variant to back-file: "Snick" + Stik/Pande compounds suggesting Whisper's struggle with the partial-acronym "SNCC" + "office" stem ("Snick office" → "Stiklpande"). // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

Pass-2 #34.P2T.1 catches this; Pass 3 cross-references to catalog #C "Foreman / Jim Foreman → James Forman" — the doubled-vowel-insertion + common-noun-substitution pattern. New variant to back-file: "former" (homonym substitution, distinct from "Foreman" doubled-vowel form). // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
Two distinct Whisper renderings for the same canonical NCC figure; back-file to catalog #C as a new entry. // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
